files_modified.summary: Added GrpcEntityEdgeClient (real LookupEntity/PutEntity/PutEdge client) and GrpcSegmentWiringClient (composes GrpcPutSegmentClient + GrpcEntityEdgeClient into one full SegmentWiringClient). Updated module docstring's 'Real vs. Protocol-only RPC surface' section and SegmentWiringClient's/GrpcPutSegmentClient's docstrings to reflect that the gap is now closed. No changes to execute_segment's logic, SegmentWiringClient's Protocol method signatures, TopicNotFoundError, PutSegmentResult, SegmentExecutionResult, or GrpcPutSegmentClient's existing put_segment behavior.

Added 7 new tests covering GrpcEntityEdgeClient's three methods (request/response translation, default weight_delta, RPC-error propagation) and GrpcSegmentWiringClient (delegation across all four methods, and one end-to-end execute_segment test against the composed real-shaped client). Updated module docstring's bullet list and imports. No existing test modified or removed.

downstream_consumers.agents/ingestion/wiring.py: No known callers yet wire GrpcSegmentWiringClient into a production entrypoint -- that integration (constructing a real grpc.Channel and calling execute_segment against it end-to-end, e.g. from an ingestion pipeline/CLI) is not part of 3.4.4b's scope per the task brief's impacted-module list, and is expected to land as part of subtask 3.4.6 (fixture suite + live-Ollama smoke test).

risk_assessment.backward_compatibility: Fully additive: two new classes added, no existing public name removed or resignatured. SegmentWiringClient Protocol's method signatures unchanged, so any existing test double or caller keeps working unmodified.
risk_assessment.test_coverage_delta: 14 -> 21 tests in test_segment_wiring.py (+7). Full agents/ suite: 120 -> 127 (+7), matching exactly.
risk_assessment.runtime_risk: None observed against a live engine (out of scope for this subtask per task brief point 5's testing approach -- mocked channel/stub only, no live server). grpc.RpcError-shaped propagation confirmed at the client level; execute_segment's pre-existing broad except Exception clauses were already sufficient and needed no change.
